current_blocker: Suppressed as validated duplicate of V12 High 'Collateral can be enabled despite pause/freeze or invalid pricing'. Local PoC adds inactive-reserve/borrow evidence but shares the same root cause and affected transfer/collateral-enable path.
duplicate_check: V12 High #44815: Collateral can be enabled despite pause/freeze or invalid pricing; same targets ATokenContract::transfer_internal and KineticRouter::validate_and_finalize_transfer.
false_positive_risk: _beforeTokenTransfer whitelist check gap is related to role-state consistency, but the final report classified it as low absent a stronger value-loss path; address(0) blacklist interaction is a configuration edge case that blocks burns rather than enabling unauthorized asset extraction; native collateral minting unavailability is an asset-configuration limitation while redemption remains available; no direct theft path is shown
final_decision: SUPPRESSED-DUPLICATE: validated locally but not eligible for C4 HM submission.
text: aToken.transfer_internal calls router.validate_and_finalize_transfer, but that router hook validates caller/HF/collateral bitmaps and does not enforce global pool pause or reserve paused/active flags. Direct aToken transfers may continue moving collateral shares during emergency pause/reserve pause/inactive state.
title: aToken transfers bypass pool/reserve pause and inactive state

current_blocker: Suppressed by known signature 'missing self authorization breaks cross contract protocol flows' plus adjacent flash liquidation known findings.
duplicate_check: known-finding-signatures.txt line 61: missing self authorization breaks cross contract protocol flows; lines 46-48/106 include flash-liquidation known families.
final_decision: SUPPRESSED-DUPLICATE/WEAK: do not package as HM.
text: execute_liquidation_callback invokes SEP-41 debt_asset.transfer from the pool/current contract to aToken, treasury, and liquidator without env.authorize_as_current_contract. This can break protocol-owned token transfers in the flash liquidation callback, but known signatures already include missing self authorization breaks cross contract protocol flows and multiple flash-liquidation issues.
title: flash liquidation callback transfers debt asset without self authorization

current_blocker: Cold TTL lead is real as a storage/expiration mismatch pattern across Token/AToken allowance keys, but K2 known signatures already include missing expiration validation and off-by-one allowance expiration; impact is early approval loss/integrator DoS, not a protocol asset-loss path. Suppress as QA/duplicate-risk.
text: Base Token approve accepts arbitrary expiration_ledger but storage::set_allowance only extends persistent Allowance(from,spender) to fixed 365-day TTL. A long-lived SEP-41 allowance can disappear before its declared expiration if nobody touches the key, causing transfer_from/burn_from or integrator standing approvals to fail early. Duplicate/QA risk: allowance family already has off-by-one and missing expiration validation; impact appears integration DoS rather than direct K2 asset loss.
title: token allowance storage ttl shorter than expiration ledger

current_blocker: refresh_prices is public and clears LastPriceData before refetch, but failed Soroban invocations roll back storage writes; successful refresh only replaces cache with fresh validated data. Remaining effect is public cache-warming/fee grief at most and overlaps oracle cache known families, not submission-ready HM.
text: PriceOracleContract::refresh_prices is public and unauthenticated. It clears LastPriceData for every supplied asset before calling get_asset_prices_vec; if the later fetch fails on a stale/disabled/unwhitelisted/malformed asset, the transaction reverts on Soroban but if storage changes are rolled back then no persistent grief exists. Need validate rollback semantics; likely QA/false-positive unless cache eviction persists or causes user-facing DoS.
title: public refresh_prices can evict oracle cache before reverting

current_blocker: V12 Med/Low line ~45018 explicitly covers public update_reserve_state path-dependent compounding cadence; not novel.
text: KineticRouter exposes update_reserve_state as a public unauthenticated entrypoint; it persists liquidity_index, variable_borrow_index and last_update_timestamp, so a third party can choose intermediate accrual boundaries. Duplicate check shows V12 already covers this path-dependent compounding issue.
title: public update_reserve_state can choose reserve compounding cadence

current_blocker: V12 Med/Low line ~12070/~13547 explicitly covers false total_borrow <= total_supply invariant in get_protocol_reserves/collect_protocol_reserves.
text: calculation::get_protocol_reserves still returns InvalidAmount if total_borrow > total_supply before computing raw reserves/deficit, which can break reserve views/collection in insolvent or high utilization states. Duplicate check shows V12 Med/Low already covers the false total_borrow <= total_supply invariant.
title: get_protocol_reserves reverts when total debt exceeds total supply

current_blocker: V12 Critical covers calculate_user_account_data_unified trusting TTL-managed user_config/reserve counter/default-zero state; no independent non-TTL root cause found.
text: calculate_user_account_data_unified trusts user_config bits and reserve id lookups; if reserve-id/config TTL/corruption makes active positions disappear, debt/collateral accounting can be zeroed or skipped. Duplicate check shows V12 Critical already covers TTL-managed user_config/reserve counter/default-zero accounting failures.
title: account data skips active config positions when reserve id lookup is missing

current_blocker: 需要用当前目标仓库代码验证 reachability、权限边界和资产影响，不能只依赖历史相似案例; 需要排除 caution/false-positive 通道召回的降级条件
false_positive_risk: the public Silo/Cantina report treated this as Low/Informational because the scenario depended on economically unlikely 1 wei collateral representing meaningful value and no working PoC was produced; native collateral minting unavailability is an asset-configuration limitation while redemption remains available; no direct theft path is shown; address(0) blacklist interaction is a configuration edge case that blocks burns rather than enabling unauthorized asset extraction
text: Soroban lending debt token transfer after repay may reuse stale collateral side cache and corrupt solvency configuration
title: external report sweep smoke: stale solvency transfer

current_blocker: Static review found real-looking parity drift, but no fresh HM path yet: LiquidationEngineContract::execute_liquidation accepts _receive_a_token but forwards false to router liquidation_call, while its local calculate/get_max helpers use engine-local close factor/HF-only calculations. However K2 known signatures already cover engine uncapped debt, close-factor/dust liquidation families, reserve ACL bypass, stale liquidation authorization, and settlement mode ignored. Needs a distinct non-duplicate asset-loss path before revival.
duplicate_check: Overlaps known signatures: engine uses uncapped debt for executed liquidation; close factor bypass via dust clamping; dust leftover check unconditionally panics allowing attackers to block liquidations; liquidation flow bypasses reserve level access controls; settlement mode always ignored; stale liquidation authorization allows liquidating healthy positions.
false_positive_risk: If execute_liquidation is only an auxiliary integration path and the router liquidation_call still enforces liquidator auth plus router-level validations, _receive_a_token mismatch may be UX/settlement parity rather than protocol loss. Helper view divergence is not enough without users/contracts relying on it for enforced settlement.
final_decision: Suppress for now; keep as internal Wise Lending-inspired liquidation parity note unless a new PoC proves a distinct asset-loss or liquidation-blocking path not covered by V12/known signatures.
text: Using new Wise Lending liquidation cases, inspect K2 liquidation-engine execute_liquidation: it accepts _receive_a_token but forwards false to kinetic-router liquidation_call, and its helper get_max_liquidatable_debt/is_position_liquidatable use engine-local close factor/HF-only logic that may diverge from router execution guards. Need determine whether this is only UX/integration parity or a submission-worthy liquidation path issue.
title: liquidation engine ignores receive_a_token and has helper parity drift

current_blocker: Confirmed after re-triage/suppress-check: static MAX_USER_RESERVES guard gap is real, but suppress-check still recommends suppress-or-duplicate-review. Needs PoC proving 16th reserve via swap_collateral plus non-self/protocol-level impact; otherwise self-fragmentation/QA and adjacent to known 'unsolicited dust transfers can fill all active reserve slots'. RAG high-severity matches are generic lending/accounting analogies, not direct K2 proof.
duplicate_check: Adjacent known signature: unsolicited dust transfers can fill all active reserve slots; also reserve cap bypass freezes liquidation / wrong active slot check families in known-finding-signatures.txt.
false_positive_risk: _beforeTokenTransfer whitelist check gap is related to role-state consistency, but the final report classified it as low absent a stronger value-loss path; the public Silo/Cantina report treated this as Low/Informational because the scenario depended on economically unlikely 1 wei collateral representing meaningful value and no working PoC was produced; dual whitelist/blacklist role assignment is an admin hygiene issue unless it creates a demonstrated value-moving bypass
text: K2 kinetic-router swap_collateral sets the to_asset collateral bit unconditionally after minting swapped collateral and only disables from_asset when new_user_scaled_balance == 0. Unlike supply/borrow/liquidation/aToken transfer finalize paths, it does not check count_active_reserves() against MAX_USER_RESERVES before adding a new destination reserve. Need determine whether partial swaps let a user exceed the 15 active-reserve cap, and whether impact is only self-account fragmentation/known dust-slot family or a distinct protocol-level DoS/accounting issue.
title: swap_collateral can add a destination collateral bit without MAX_USER_RESERVES check

text: Swept code-rag hook/callback transitional-state pattern against K2 flash_loan and flash liquidation. External flash_loan callback occurs while protocol-wide router reentrancy guard remains held, so guarded value-moving entrypoints cannot be reentered. Internal flash liquidation validates again at execute time before callback, callback is internal when receiver == pool, and post-callback state uses scaled totals returned by token burns. Unguarded validate_and_finalize_transfer can be called by aToken during callbacks, but it requires aToken auth and only syncs transfer bitmaps; observed paths do not give attacker a non-duplicate protocol-loss mutation beyond known global flash loan state / two-step flash liquidation / stale authorization families. Keep suppressed unless a PoC shows a whitelisted handler can mutate borrower balances after LIQSCL snapshot to cause protocol loss, not self-grief.
title: Hook/callback transitional state in flash loan paths appears covered by router lock

current_blocker: PoC validated. Main remaining risk is duplicate framing: closest known signatures are 'liquidation flow bypasses reserve level access controls' and 'stale liquidation authorization'. Keep scope narrow: collateral reserve pause after prepare; do not claim stale HF/healthy-account liquidation or debt reserve bypass.
duplicate_check: Known signatures reviewed. Adjacent: liquidation flow bypasses reserve level access controls; stale liquidation authorization allows liquidating healthy positions; global pause does not stop flash loans. Candidate is distinct only if reserve-local lifecycle pause between TX1/TX2 is not already covered.
false_positive_risk: execute_liquidation does recheck HF and prices, so impact is not healthy-account liquidation. Severity relies on emergency reserve-local pause semantics being expected to block collateral seizure/settlement after prepare.
text: K2 two-step flash liquidation: prepare_liquidation validates collateral/debt reserve active and paused state before storing authorization, but execute_liquidation later refetches current reserves without repeating collateral reserve active/paused lifecycle validation before using callback params. Local PoC pauses the collateral reserve after prepare; fresh prepare is blocked while old execute_liquidation succeeds. Candidate is Medium but duplicate risk must be framed narrowly against known liquidation flow reserve-level access controls/stale authorization families.
title: Prepared liquidations can execute against a collateral reserve paused after authorization

current_blocker: Local fake-asset PoC demonstrates reward drain only after emission_manager configures attacker-controlled asset, or after unauthenticated initialize/front-run. That is privileged configuration / known public-initializer territory, not a clean non-privileged HM. Keep as QA/duplicate-risk note unless a non-emission-manager route is found.
false_positive_risk: missing oracle sanity checks are low unless invalid reward/slash values are reachable from untrusted sources and affect solvency; _beforeTokenTransfer whitelist check gap is related to role-state consistency, but the final report classified it as low absent a stronger value-loss path; 如果该 helper 不是公开 entrypoint，且所有公开调用者已经对同一个被扣款/被 claim 的 Address 完成 require_auth，则 helper 内不重复授权本身不是漏洞。
text: IncentivesContract stores lending_pool as authorized handle_action caller, but handle_action only token_address.require_auth() and never checks get_lending_pool. configure_asset_rewards lets emission_manager register arbitrary asset addresses. A fake asset contract configured as reward asset can call handle_action as itself with arbitrary total_supply/user_balance, accrue rewards for attacker, then claim real reward_token from incentives balance. Local PoC poc_fake_asset_contract_can_drain_funded_rewards passes in k2-unit-tests.
title: Fake configured incentives asset can mint and drain funded rewards
